get.summary: List Registrations Eligible For Deletion For Admin
get.description:
Lists registrations eligible for deletion for an organization (paginated).

    Eligibility requires that **every** associated filing has status UNFILED, or the
    registration has no filings. If **any** filing has a different status (FILED, FILING,
    etc.), or filings have **mixed** statuses, the registration is **not** returned.

    By default, registrations with **no** filings are included (``filings_count_to_delete``
    is 0). Set ``include_registrations_with_no_filings=false`` to return only registrations
    that have at least one filing to delete (UNFILED rows only in this list).

    Use standard ``page`` and ``size`` query parameters for pagination (see response
    ``total`` and ``pages``).

    Each item includes jurisdiction, registration status, filings count, and nested filings
    (all UNFILED when non-empty).

parameters.name: include_registrations_with_no_filings

schema.description: If false, omit registrations with zero filings (only rows with UNFILED filings to delete).

get.summary: Debug DNS resolution and URL reachability
get.description:
Test DNS resolution and URL reachability from the Lambda environment.

    This endpoint helps debug which DNS records the Lambda can see and which
    URLs are reachable. It performs both HTTP requests to test connectivity
    and DNS lookups using dig/nslookup commands.

    Parameters:
    - urls: Comma-separated list of URLs to test (e.g., "https://example.com,api.service.com")
    - hostnames: Comma-separated list of hostnames for DNS lookup (e.g., "example.com,api.service.com")
    - dns_method: DNS lookup method to use ("dig" or "nslookup", defaults to "dig")
    - timeout: Request timeout in seconds (defaults to 10)

Dispatches by ``object_type``:

- ``SODOCUMENT`` / ``ARINVOICE`` → invoice tax estimate + Sage write-back
- ``ITEM`` → product import (no Sage write-back)
- ``CUSTOMER`` → customer import (no Sage write-back)

API key may be sent as the ``X-API-KEY`` HTTP header or as a form/JSON field
(``X-API-KEY`` / ``api_key``) when Sage cannot set custom headers.

Returns 202 immediately; processing runs in a background task.

get.description:
Finalize the SHOPLINE OAuth dance: verify state/HMAC, persist tokens.

``shop`` may be omitted on some SHOPLINE redirects (e.g. ``handle`` +
``sign`` only); the service then derives the store domain from the
handle stored in the CSRF cache payload. When ``shop`` is present it
must match that derived domain. SHOPLINE may send either ``hmac``
(Shopify-style) or ``sign`` for the query MAC; if both are present,
``hmac`` wins.

SHOPLINE's documented OAuth callback echoes ``customField`` (set on the
consent URL) rather than a standard ``state`` parameter; the router
forwards ``state`` **or** ``customField`` as the CSRF cache key.
The service accepts one of them; both missing or invalid yields a
**redirect** to the app (typically ``/integrations`` with an error
status), not HTTP ``400`` — unlike ``GET /shopline/install``, which
maps :class:`ShoplineAuthenticationError` to ``400`` for a bad
App-Market first hop.

Handle a SHOPLINE-initiated install: verify HMAC, 302 to consent.

SHOPLINE's App Market redirects the merchant here when they click
*install*. The first hop carries ``timestamp`` plus either Shopify-style
``shop`` + ``hmac`` or SHOPLINE-native ``handle`` + ``sign`` (and often
``appkey``); if both ``hmac`` and ``sign`` are present, ``hmac`` wins.
All are verified inside the service layer (HMAC, then freshness via
:data:`services.oauth._INSTALL_TIMESTAMP_TOLERANCE_SECONDS`).

The post-signup round-trip is a special case: the merchant returns
with a server-issued ``shopline_signup_token`` instead of a fresh
SHOPLINE signature. That path reuses the install context we
verified on the first hop, so it doesn't need (and mustn't require)
a new HMAC. We therefore declare all three fields optional at the
router level and let the service enforce the actual invariant —
"either a valid signup token OR (timestamp + verified HMAC/sign with shop or handle)."

post.description:
Receive all SHOPLINE webhook deliveries and dispatch by topic.
Order of operations:

1. HMAC-verify the raw body (covers only the body, not headers).
2. Parse JSON.
3. Resolve the shop handle from the **signed** body, cross-checked
   against the unsigned ``X-Shopline-Shop-Domain`` header — never
   trust the header alone.

The topic is taken from ``X-Shopline-Topic``; the registered callback
URL is the same for every topic (see ``register_webhooks``).

ApideckWebhookEvent.title: ApideckWebhookEvent
ApideckWebhookEvent.description:
Enumeration of possible HRIS webhook events.

Attributes:
    EMPLOYEE_CREATED: Triggered when a new employee record is created.
    EMPLOYEE_UPDATED: Triggered when an existing employee record is updated.
    EMPLOYEE_DELETED: Triggered when an employee record is deleted.
    EMPLOYEE_TERMINATED: Triggered when an employee is terminated.
    ACCOUNTING_INVOICE_CREATED: Triggered when a new invoice is created.
    ACCOUNTING_INVOICE_UPDATED: Triggered when an existing invoice is updated.
    ACCOUNTING_INVOICE_DELETED: Triggered when an invoice is deleted.
    ACCOUNTING_INVOICE_ITEM_CREATED: Triggered when a new invoice item is created.
    VAULT_CONNECTION_CREATED: Triggered when a new vault connection is created.
    VAULT_CONNECTION_UPDATED:
        Triggered when an existing vault connection is updated.
    VAULT_CONNECTION_DISABLED: Triggered when a vault connection is disabled.
    VAULT_CONNECTION_DELETED: Triggered when a vault connection is deleted.
    VAULT_CONNECTION_CALLABLE:
        Triggered when a vault connection becomes callable aka authorized.
    VAULT_CONNECTION_REVOKED: Triggered when a vault connection is revoked.
    VAULT_CONNECTION_TOKEN_REFRESH_FAILED:
        Triggered when token refresh fails for a vault connection.

For example, if the value is 2022-10-28T15:36:28.129+05:30, then the timestamp represents October 28, 2022, at 15:36:28.129, with an offset of +05:30. In the case of a merchant site located in UTC, these datatypes would send a timestamp in the format 2022-11-11T15:40:44.65Z. PublicRequestStatusResponse.title: PublicRequestStatusResponse
PublicRequestStatusResponse.description:
Public landing-page payload. Mirrors PublicRequestStatusDTO from
public_service.py.

`request_status_pill` is the **request-level** rollup the FE uses to
drive the confirmation card headline + "What happens next" timeline.
Before this field existed, the FE picked the latest cert's pill,
which flipped to "Exemption Applied" the moment any single cert was
approved — even when other requested jurisdictions still had no
coverage. The pill values mirror the per-cert pill plus
`PARTIALLY_COMPLETED` for the multi-juris partial-approval case
(Research doc Decision #8: `Sent → In Review → Partially Completed |
Completed | Rejected`).

A request-level `requested_certificate_type` was considered but is
out-of-scope for V2 (V-100 work; see research doc). Each cert's
`extracted_certificate_type` (on `_UploadedCertificateResponse`) is
the OCR-extracted cert type and stays on the per-cert response.

Internal metadata (raw status enum, request_id, customer/org names,
notes, expires_at) is intentionally not exposed on this public
surface — the rollup pill is a derived value, not the raw enum.
